summary: VibePulse backend end-to-end tested via pytest against public URL https://pulse-connect-52.preview.emergentagent.com. All 37 test cases pass (100% success). Coverage: auth (register/login/me/duplicate/invalid creds/JWT guards), profile update + public user with top_post, posts CRUD (280-char validation, feed hydration, like toggle, comments, user posts), discover (self + swiped exclusion, orientation filter), swipes (self-swipe 400, nonexistent target 404, duplicate upsert), mutual-like match creation (idempotent), matches list, chat (send/receive/sorted asc/inbound read-marking/stranger 404), auth guards on 7 protected endpoints, and AI moderation (fail-open behavior verified).

minor.issue: Login with malformed email returns 422 (Pydantic EmailStr) rather than 401. Minor — spec says invalid credentials should be 401. Currently only strictly-formatted emails that don't exist return 401. Consider making Login model accept plain `str` for email so unknown-format inputs also yield 401 (avoids user-enumeration channel via error code).
Uses model_dump(exclude_unset=True) then filters `v is not None`, meaning clients cannot explicitly clear a field by sending null. Not a bug, just a design note.

action_items: No blocking action items. Optional: make Login endpoint's email field a plain str (not EmailStr) so bad-format emails also return 401, matching spec wording 'invalid credentials => 401'.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~650 lines — still manageable but nearing the 700-line threshold; consider splitting into routers/auth.py, routers/posts.py, routers/swipes.py, routers/chat.py before adding more features.

Match creation uses `db.matches.find_one({'user_ids': {'$all': [...]}} )` for idempotency but there is no unique index on the sorted user_ids pair. Under a race (two mutual-like requests landing simultaneously) two match docs could be created. Recommend a unique index on `user_ids` (sorted).

`toggle_like` returns `max(0, likes_count - 1)` for the unlike branch which masks any drift; server-side count could drift under concurrent likes because $inc/$pull are two separate ops (not atomic together). Consider using aggregation pipeline update to keep likes_count = size(likes) atomically.

`moderate_text` initializes a new LlmChat per call — fine for MVP but adds latency (~1-2s per post/comment/message). Consider a lightweight in-process moderation cache for identical text or async fire-and-forget review + soft-flag.
`get_current_user` fetches from DB on every request — acceptable but consider caching or minimizing project fields for hot paths (feed).
The @app.on_event('startup')/('shutdown') decorators are deprecated in favor of FastAPI lifespan handlers; not urgent but worth migrating.
seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_* users at @vibetest.app (uuid-suffixed) and re-used seed users alice@vibe.app, bob@vibe.app, zara@vibe.app from /app/memory/test_credentials.md. Users' profiles were normalized (gender/orientation/onboarded=true) via PUT /api/users/me at fixture setup so discover/swipe flows behave deterministically.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test suite lives at /app/backend/tests/. Run: `cd /app/backend/tests && pytest -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/vibepulse_results.xml`. Fixtures in conftest.py use the public EXPO_BACKEND_URL. Note: Pydantic EmailStr rejects the `.test` TLD (RFC 6761 special-use) — always use `.app`, `.com`, or `.io` for synthetic test emails. Mutual-match test uses `pytest.match_pair` module attribute to pass state to downstream chat/matches-list tests; keep them in order (TestSwipesAndMatch → TestMatchesList → TestChat).
rca_of_issue: N/A — no functional bugs found. All 37 assertions passed. AI moderation returned SAFE for profane test text (fail-open OK per spec).
